name: Verde Sierra de la Demanda — circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda
name_original: Verde Sierra de la Demanda — circular por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda
region_intro: Hiking around Barbadillo De Herreros offers exploration within the Sierra de la Demanda mountain range, characterized by its mountainous terrain and dense forests of oak, beech, and pine. The Pedroso River flows through the area, contributing to a lush environment and featuring waterfalls like Cascada del Chorranco. Prominent elevations include Pico San Millán, the highest point in Burgos at 2,132 meters, and the Alto de Pradilla mountain pass. Best hiking trails around Barbadillo De Herreros The most popular hiking route is Trail of the Waterfalls, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route leads through ancient forests and past scenic waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to San Millan and Cabeza Aguilez — circular through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space, a difficult 11.5 miles (18.6 km) path. This challenging route offers panoramic views from the summit of Pico San Millán. Local hikers also love the Verde Sierra de la Demanda — circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra de la Demanda Natural Space, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Hiking around Barbadillo De Herreros is defined by mountainous terrain, dense forests, and river valleys with waterfalls. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest paths to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Barbadillo De Herreros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Barbadillo De Herreros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Barbadillo De Herreros?
A: Barbadillo De Herreros offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 distinct trails available. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Barbadillo De Herreros?
A: The region is beautiful throughout the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter brings the possibility of snow in the higher parts of the Sierra de la Demanda, making some routes more challenging.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in the area?
A: Yes, Barbadillo De Herreros has several easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. There are 5 easy trails, often following river valleys or less steep terrain, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty without significant challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly walks in Barbadillo De Herreros?
A: Many of the easier trails are suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041602606/trail-of-the-waterfalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that can be enjoyed by families, leading through ancient forests and past scenic waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041618230/verde-sierra-de-la-demanda-circular-by-the-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verde Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt; also offers accessible sections.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Barbadillo De Herreros?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in Barbadillo De Herreros and the Sierra de la Demanda. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to ensure you clean up after them. Some protected areas might have specific rules, so check local signage.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Barbadillo De Herreros?
A: Yes, the region offers several excellent circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041618230/verde-sierra-de-la-demanda-circular-by-the-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-la-demanda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verde Sierra de la Demanda — circular by the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de la Demanda&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through diverse landscapes. Another notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041620202/ascent-to-san-millan-and-cabeza-aguilez-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-demanda-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to San Millan and Cabeza Aguilez&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging circular route for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails in Barbadillo De Herreros showcase the diverse beauty of the Sierra de la Demanda. You'll encounter dense forests of ancient oak, beech, and pine, the flowing Pedroso River, and picturesque waterfalls like the Cascada del Chorranco and Cascada del río Pedroso. Higher routes offer panoramic views from peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover historical remnants of the region's mining past, including old ironworks. For those seeking panoramic views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4539513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Pradilla&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass offers stunning vistas. The challenging ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico San Millán&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in Burgos, is also a significant landmark.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Barbadillo De Herreros?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush river valleys and ancient forests to challenging mountain ascents, and the well-maintained trails that offer a true immersion in nature.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041602606/trail-of-the-waterfalls" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail of the Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to highlight these natural wonders, leading you past several stunning cascades like the Cascada del Chorranco and Cascada del río Pedroso.

Q: What is the difficulty level of the trails in Barbadillo De Herreros?
A: The trails vary significantly in difficulty. You'll find 5 easy routes for gentle walks, 22 moderate trails suitable for most hikers, and 16 difficult routes, including challenging ascents to peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico San Millán&lt;/a&gt;, which require good fitness and experience.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging hikes?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041620202/ascent-to-san-millan-and-cabeza-aguilez-circular-through-the-sierra-de-la-demanda-natural-space" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to San Millan and Cabeza Aguilez&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and panoramic rewards. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enrique Rivero Trail – San Millán (2,131 m) loop from Refugio de Zárcia&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Riocavado de la Sierra is a town and a municipality located in the province of Burgos, autonomous community of Castilla y León (Spain), region of La Demanda, judicial district of Salas de los Infantes, head of the town hall of his name. In 2014 it had a population of 57 inhabitants.
Within the Via Verda de la Demanda there is a red bridge that avoids the torrent that goes in the direction of Riocavado de la Sierra.
Wonderfully varied bike tour on an old railway line in the middle of the mountains. Easy-to-drive route, gentle slope, great views - what more could you want.
